title: deer-flow.research.cycle.completed v1
description: Emitted by deer-flow's ResearchDispatchConsumer when a meta_probe cycle completes. hearth-api (nbus_consumer.rs::handle_research_cycle_completed) writes the result to ~/knowledge/indexed/deer-flow/<research_id>.md and upserts cycle_results + action_items tables. Note: the kb_path field is stale and ignored by hearth-api; do not rely on it to locate the written file.

research_id.description: Echoed from the dispatch event — the caller's correlation ID.

cycle_id.description: deer-flow's internal cycle identifier (e.g. '20260525T193105Z-3924'). Maps to a results directory under skills/custom/stack-tuner/results/.

question.description: The original research question, echoed for consumer convenience.

question_class.description: The question class used for this cycle.

summary.description: The full summary.json payload from the completed cycle. Key fields: verdict (ship|lift_measured|retry_bulk_recommended|bulk_failed), state (completed|failed), final_answer, best_answer, cumulative_cost_usd, wall_elapsed_s.

kb_path.description: DEPRECATED — stale field, ignored by hearth-api. hearth-api::handle_research_cycle_completed always writes to ~/knowledge/indexed/deer-flow/<research_id>.md regardless of this value. Do not use this field to locate the written KB entry. The old example path '~/projects/kb/home-automation/{research_id}.yaml' is incorrect.
